Okay, so now I want to talk about types of lists because there is more than one type of list. So first of all, two really big categories. They could go out of business, whatever. And also the other thing is there's just nowhere near as responsive. I mean, I would rather have a 1,000 person email list than 10,000 followers on YouTube or Facebook. You just, the 1,000 email subscribers are going to respond more than in social media. It's just the way it is. So the, you know, yeah, build followers in all those social platforms or whatever ones you want to operate in, but realize that the real goal is to take people from there and drive them over to your email list. ## [03:01 - 06:02]

Clients are worth a lot more. In fact, in my experience, in different markets and different niches, the number that I keep on coming back to is someone who has bought from you already, who is on your client list, is worth 15 times more than someone who's just opted in than a prospect. And that can vary. I'm sure there are some lists that it's different. Some markets it's different. But my experience is it's amazing how consistent this has been. It's 15 times more valuable for clients and buyers. Now another list you can have is JVs, which is Joint Venture Partners or Affiliates. And really, for our purposes, we're just going to say those two things are equivalent. I think of them as a little bit different, like Joint Venture Partners are people I have a closer relationship to. And the funny thing about Joint Venture Partners or Affiliates is that 95% of them aren't going to do any good. They'll never promote for you, never make any sales. But the people who do, like the ones who are really engaged here, they might be worth 500 times more than a client is worth. I mean, I might have a partner that's doing hundreds of thousands of dollars in sales for with me. So these can be really, really powerful. So obviously, when you have these three different lists, you have to talk to people and you have to talk to them differently. You don't have the same relationship with these three. And you have to understand that basically, they're different avatars. The people on here, they are fundamentally different than your prospects. And your affiliates are completely different from your clients. But in a case, three major types of lists, and you have to understand that you have to speak to them different, that they are different avatars, that you have different relationships with them. And when someone buys from you, you want to do your best to move them off your prospect list. So like, I'll talk about list management a little later on, but generally, you know, you can basically have someone automatically remove from this list when they buy from you. ## [06:02 - 07:53]

And it will work different. We'll talk about list host later on, but it will work different depending on what you're using to host your list. But you want to move people from your client list. Once someone buys from you, you want to move them from the prospect list to the buyer's list. So for me, this happens automatically with my shopping cart. Someone buys from me, and then there's a rule that says add them to the buyer's list and remove them from the prospect list. So there are different ways to do this depending on, you know, what list host you use. I'll get the list host sent a little bit, but you definitely want to remove people from here after they join here. Now one of the things, it's never perfect, well, generally in most systems, not 100% perfect because someone might use a different email address when they buy, and then, you know, you can't remove them because you don't know what email address they're using for your prospect list. So it's not perfect, but you want to do your best to take them off that prospect list. Now, this sort of brings, this is a conceptual thing, but it's an important thing. And that's that you're the purpose of a sale. When you start operating at the level you want to be in this business, the purpose of a sale isn't to make money. The purpose of a sale is to generate a buyer, you know. You want to move them from here to here. And because of this number right here, because they're worth 15 times more in the future. So you basically, the purpose of a sale is to generate a buyer, to move them into being a client. And then you've got a different relationship with them, and you're going to be able to sell to them more in the future. And really, I mean, you can even take it a step further and say it's not the purpose of a sale isn't to generate a buyer or a client. The purpose of a sale is to generate a raving fan. And that will absolutely work for you.
